title: Undefined negative pseudo-power ((u+delta)Pi_S - tilde L_S)^p in Step 4 decomposition

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Location.** Step 4 ("Proof of (total)"), the spectral decomposition of $M^p$.

**The claim as written.** For $p\in\{-1,-2\}$ the proof writes
$$M^p \;=\; (u+\delta)^p\,\Pi_S^\perp \;+\; \bigl((u+\delta)\Pi_S-\widetilde L_S\bigr)^p .$$

**The defect.** The matrix $A_1:=(u+\delta)\Pi_S-\widetilde L_S$ is **singular**: on
$\operatorname{im}\Pi_S^\perp$ both $\Pi_S$ and $\widetilde L_S$ vanish, so $A_1$ annihilates that
subspace. Hence $A_1^{-1}$ and $A_1^{-2}$ do **not exist** in the ordinary sense, and the
symbol $A_1^{p}$ ($p<0$) is undefined as written. A strict verifier can reject the
unqualified expression.

**Intended (correct) meaning.** $A_1^p$ must be read as the **pseudo-power**: diagonalize
$A_1=\sum_i \mu_i v_iv_i^\top$ and set $A_1^p:=\sum_{\mu_i\ne0}\mu_i^p v_iv_i^\top$, i.e. the
$p$-th power on $\operatorname{im}\Pi_S=(\ker A_1)^\perp$ and $0$ on $\ker A_1$. Equivalently $A_1^p$
is the $p$-th power of the restriction $M|_{\operatorname{im}\Pi_S}$ (note $A_1=M$ on
$\operatorname{im}\Pi_S$ since $\widetilde L_S$ is supported there). Under this reading the displayed
identity is correct.

**Numerical confirmation.** With the pseudo-power convention, the identity
$M^p=(u+\delta)^p\Pi_S^\perp+A_1^p$ holds to machine precision (max error $\approx10^{-15}$)
for $p=-1,-2$ on random instances.

**Fix.** Add a one-line convention before this display: "for a singular symmetric $A$ and
$p<0$, $A^p$ denotes the pseudo-power (invert the nonzero eigenvalues, zero on $\ker A$)."
Alternatively, write $M^p$ directly via its own eigendecomposition restricted to
$\operatorname{im}\Pi_S$ and $\operatorname{im}\Pi_S^\perp$, avoiding the symbol $A_1^p$ altogether.

**Severity.** Minor / definitional. The mathematics is correct under the intended reading;
this is an exposition gap, not an error.

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
## Critic re-review (independent confirmation) â€” GAP CONFIRMED

I independently confirm this gap in the current `solution.tex`. In Step 4 the proof writes
$M^p=(u+\delta)^p\Pi_S^\perp+((u+\delta)\Pi_S-\widetilde L_S)^p$ for $p\in\{-1,-2\}$ and sets $A:=((u+\delta)\Pi_S-\widetilde L_S)^p$. The base $A_1:=(u+\delta)\Pi_S-\widetilde L_S$ is singular (it vanishes on $\operatorname{im}\Pi_S^\perp=\ker\widetilde L_S$), so a literal negative power is undefined.

**Numerical confirmation of the intended reading.** Taking $A_1^p$ as the pseudo-power on $\operatorname{im}\Pi_S$ (invert only the nonzero eigenvalues, extend by 0), I verified over 2000 random instances ($n=8$, $\sigma=3$, $p\in\{-1,-2\}$) that
$\;(u+\delta)^p\Pi_S^\perp+A_1^p=M^p\;$ and $\;\Pi_S M^p\Pi_S=A_1^p\;$ hold to machine precision. So the result is correct under the pseudo-power reading; only the notation is undefined.

**Recommended one-line fix:** define $A:=\Pi_S M^p\Pi_S$ directly (the proof already proves this equals the intended object), which simultaneously removes the undefined power and makes the subsequent Ky Fan maximum-principle step act on $\Pi_S M^p\Pi_S$ verbatim. This also closes the dependency from q6-1 (the symmetrized eq.(split) feeds on a well-defined PSD $A$). Status: still OPEN.

comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
## Fix applied â€” undefined negative power eliminated

**Root cause.** Step 4 wrote $M^p=(u+\delta)^p\Pi_S^\perp+((u+\delta)\Pi_S-\widetilde L_S)^p$ and set $A:=((u+\delta)\Pi_S-\widetilde L_S)^p$. The base $A_1:=(u+\delta)\Pi_S-\widetilde L_S$ annihilates $\operatorname{im}\Pi_S^\perp=\ker\widetilde L_S$, so for $p\in\{-1,-2\}$ the literal inverse power $A_1^p$ is undefined.

**Fix (definitional rewrite, no new convention needed).** I rewrote the block so that the *only* negative power formed is that of the genuinely invertible ambient matrix $M=(u+\delta)I-\widetilde L_S$, and the previously-undefined object is replaced by the well-defined compression $\Pi_S M^p\Pi_S$:

1. **$M$ is invertible.** Finiteness $\mathcal B^u_\sigma(S)<\infty$ forces $u>\lambda_1(\widetilde L_S)$, so $u+\delta>\lambda_i(\widetilde L_S)$ for all $i$, giving $M\succ0$. Hence $M^{-1},M^{-2}$ are ordinary symmetric PSD matrices â€” this is the only meaning attached to $M^p$.

2. **Block identity (exact, not a convention).** $\Pi_S$ is a spectral projector of $\widetilde L_S$, so $M\Pi_S=\Pi_S M$ and $M^p$ commutes with $\Pi_S$; on $\operatorname{im}\Pi_S^\perp$ it acts as $(u+\delta)^pI$. This yields the well-defined
$$M^p=(u+\delta)^p\Pi_S^\perp+\Pi_S M^p\Pi_S\qquad(\text{eq.\ Mp-block}),$$
with $\Pi_S M^p\Pi_S\succeq0$ supported on $\operatorname{im}\Pi_S$. (A parenthetical notes this equals the $p$-th power of the *positive-definite restriction* $M|_{\operatorname{im}\Pi_S}$, but the singular ambient matrix is never inverted.)

3. Propagated downstream: $A:=\Pi_S M^p\Pi_S$ replaces $((u+\delta)\Pi_S-\widetilde L_S)^p$ throughout the $\Pi_S$-piece; the identity $\Pi A\Pi=A=\Pi_S M^p\Pi_S$ now uses $\Pi\Pi_S=\Pi_S$ (from $\operatorname{im}\widetilde L_S\subseteq\operatorname{im}L$, Lemma on kernel containment); and the monotonicity step $\operatorname{Tr}(A\,\widetilde L_{S,V\setminus S})\le\operatorname{Tr}(A\Pi)$ is now justified via $A\succeq0$, $\Pi-\widetilde L_{S,V\setminus S}\succeq0$. The Ky Fan step then acts on $\Pi_S M^p\Pi_S$ verbatim, exactly as the critic recommended.

**Verification.** Numerically confirmed over thousands of random instances ($n=8,\sigma=3,p\in\{-1,-2\}$): (i) $M^p=(u+\delta)^p\Pi_S^\perp+\Pi_S M^p\Pi_S$ to machine precision, (ii) $M^p\Pi_S=\Pi_S M^p\Pi_S$ ($M$ commutes with $\Pi_S$), and (iii) the full eq.(split) inequality chain holds. No occurrence of `A_1` or `((u+delta)Pi_S - tilde L_S)^p` remains in solution.tex.

No LaTeX toolchain is installed in the workspace, so compilation was skipped; the edits are syntactically local and consistent with surrounding macros.

Resolved.
